candidates.title: Define a Policy Rule module behind the Constitution prose
candidates.problem: The Constitution is a strong narrative document, but its Interface to an agent runtime is a long prose Implementation. Callers must interpret priorities, refusals, privacy, and amendments without stable rule identifiers.
candidates.solution: Add a policy-rule appendix or companion JSON/YAML with stable rule IDs, severity, scope, and response expectations while keeping CONSTITUTION.md as the human-facing source.
candidates.benefits: Locality improves for policy changes. Leverage improves because prompts, evals, and review tooling can cite exact rules.
candidates.test_impact: Simple lint can verify every rule has an ID and every refusal category maps to a rule.
candidates.risk: Medium: avoid oversimplifying ethical principles into brittle checkboxes.
candidates.suggested_validation: manual docs review plus markdown link/rule-id lint

candidates.title: Add an Amendment Decision Record seam
candidates.problem: The document says amendments must be reviewed, but the review Interface is not defined. Future agents can edit core principles without durable rationale.
candidates.solution: Introduce lightweight amendment records under docs/adr or docs/amendments with date, changed rules, rationale, expected behavior change, and rollback concern.
candidates.benefits: Locality for governance history and Leverage for future architecture/policy reviews.
candidates.test_impact: Docs checks can require an amendment record when rule text changes.

candidates.title: Create an Agent Runtime Loading contract
candidates.problem: README claims the constitution is loaded by every AlphaHuman agent before decisions, but no Interface specifies how loading is verified, versioned, or audited.
candidates.solution: Add a runtime loading contract with version, expected prompt position, checksum, audit evidence, and failure behavior.
candidates.benefits: Locality for runtime integration assumptions and Leverage for deployment checks.
candidates.test_impact: Runtime tests can assert the checksum/version is present in agent boot artifacts.
candidates.risk: Low/medium: depends on owning agent repos.
